main_sections.title: Gilli Danda
main_sections.poem_text:
In the playground, we gather around,
With a stick and a small gilli to throw,
Gilli Danda, our game is so sound!
We play with joy, watch it go!

One, two, three, swing and hit,
With our friends, we never quit!
With every swing, we feel so free,
Oh, what fun it is, you see!

The small gilli, up high it flies,
Underneath the bright blue skies.
Laughing, playing, having fun,
Under the golden rays of the sun!
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem is a joyful, communal celebration of Gilli Danda, a traditional Indian outdoor game. Its central theme is the pleasure of outdoor play, friendship, and the spirit of never giving up. The tone is energetic and exuberant, with short rhyming lines that mimic the quick, rhythmic actions of the game. Dominant imagery includes the gilli flying into bright blue skies, golden sunrays, and children laughing together — painting an outdoor scene full of movement and light. The refrain 'we never quit' carries a subtle motivational message. The poem works as both a playful description of the game and a gentle ode to the cultural and emotional value of traditional play.

question_bank.stem: Which game does the poem mention? What are the things needed to play this game?
According to the poem, how do the players feel while playing Gilli Danda?
What happens to the tiny gilli when the stick strikes it?
Which lines tell us that the children are playing the game outside?
What is the meaning of the line 'our game is so sound'?
Which word in the poem shows 'happiness'?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think + Let us Speak + Let us Listen

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Think and discuss.
1. Do you enjoy playing outdoors? Why or why not?
2. Name five outdoor games that you enjoy playing with your friends.
3. Imagine you are the small gilli. What would you see while flying up in the sky?

question_bank.stem: Do you enjoy playing outdoors? Why or why not?
Name five outdoor games that you enjoy playing with your friends.
Imagine you are the small gilli. What would you see while flying up in the sky?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Speak: Gilli Danda is a fun outdoor game. Think of a game played in groups. Share the information with your classmates. You may include: 1. The name of the game. 2. Whether it is an indoor or outdoor game. 3. Number of players needed. 4. Material required for the game. 5. Rules of the game along with safety instructions.

Let us Listen: Listen to the teacher and respond with a matching action.
1. 'Swing and hit': Pretend to swing an invisible danda.
2. 'Watch it go!': Raise your arms as if you have just hit the gilli and you are watching it fly.
3. 'Laughing, playing': Giggle or clap hands.
4. 'Shade your eyes from the sun': Keep your hands above your eyebrows.

Sameer was unhappy because he was unable to find his drawing book. He decided to redo his work. Both 'un-' and 're-' are prefixes. When we add a prefix at the beginning of a word it changes that word's meaning. Un: not; Re: again.

A. Add correct prefixes to the following words to create new words. Write the new word along with its meaning.
write, aware, comfortable, arrange, think

question_bank.stem: Add a prefix to 'write' and write the new word with its meaning.
Add a prefix to 'aware' and write the new word with its meaning.
Add a prefix to 'comfortable' and write the new word with its meaning.
Add a prefix to 'arrange' and write the new word with its meaning.
Add a prefix to 'think' and write the new word with its meaning.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. In the following table, encircle the nouns that you can see, taste, touch, or smell. The words you have NOT encircled are abstract nouns.

joy | leaf | anger | mountain
sun | shirt | toffee | rose
ice cube | sugar | hat | truth
chessboard | lie | football | deer
tree | happiness | cat | spoon
river | fear | cup | tiger

Can you think of a few more abstract nouns? Write them in your notebook and share with the class.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: 6×4 word grid with nouns (concrete and abstract) to sort by encircling concrete ones

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Fill in the blanks with the most suitable word from the list: theirs, mine, yours, his, hers, ours.
1. These crayons belong to me. These are ......................... .
2. The toy train belongs to Mudit. The toy train is ......................... .
3. The house with the red roof belongs to my cousins. The house is ......................... .
4. The little girl has bought a set of bowls to play Jal Tarang. The bowls belong to her. The bowls are ......................... .
5. We have a cow with large brown eyes. The cow is ......................... .
6. You should take better care of your things. After all, they belong to you. The things are ......................... .

question_bank.stem: These crayons belong to me. These are ________________.
The toy train belongs to Mudit. The toy train is ________________.
The house with the red roof belongs to my cousins. The house is ________________.
The little girl has bought a set of bowls to play Jal Tarang. The bowls belong to her. The bowls are ________________.
We have a cow with large brown eyes. The cow is ________________.
You should take better care of your things. After all, they belong to you. The things are ________________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Find the meanings of the following words using a dictionary.
1. Quit  2. Gather  3. Swing  4. Underneath  5. Golden
Now, use each word in a sentence of your own.

Find the meaning of 'Gather' and use it in a sentence.
Find the meaning of 'Swing' and use it in a sentence.
Find the meaning of 'Underneath' and use it in a sentence.
Find the meaning of 'Golden' and use it in a sentence.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Rearrange the following to form a question and write them in your notebook. Remember to put a question mark at the end.
• are cows where the
• is your house which
• it time is what
• the blue why sky is
• the many are how birds on tree
• you my when return will book
• walk you to do school
• a can fly kite you

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. The word 'gilli' has double 'l' in it. Similarly, the words 'millet' and 'bullock' have double 'l'. Now write three words having each of the double letters given below.
tt, pp, ee, mm, bb, rr, nn, dd, ss, gg

D. Circle the word that is different in meaning from the underlined word.
1. Miibi went to the market and bought an expensive toy. (precious / costly / cheap)
2. Anju and Farida forgot to buy popcorn and juice for the picnic. (remembered / revised / ignored)
3. The moon continued to count the stars night after night. (stopped / began / started)
4. I always complete my homework on time. (often / never / sometimes)
5. There was excitement in the shop among the toys. (interest / enjoyment / boredom)
6. Badal nervously told his mother about the dog he found on his way home. (sadly / joyfully / confidently)
7. Everyone liked the vibrant colours of the bangles. (dull / bright / shiny)
8. The fort is known for its unique style of construction. (different / unusual / common)

question_bank.stem: Circle the word different in meaning from 'expensive': precious / costly / cheap
Circle the word different in meaning from 'forgot': remembered / revised / ignored
Circle the word different in meaning from 'continued': stopped / began / started
Circle the word different in meaning from 'always': often / never / sometimes
Circle the word different in meaning from 'excitement': interest / enjoyment / boredom
Circle the word different in meaning from 'nervously': sadly / joyfully / confidently
Circle the word different in meaning from 'vibrant': dull / bright / shiny
Circle the word different in meaning from 'unique': different / unusual / common

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Just for Fun

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Let us make a Gilli Danda using waste material.
Things required: A thick, firm twig or a wooden stick (for the danda); Thick small twig (for the gilli); A rough stone (for smoothening the sticks); Colours or coloured papers (optional, for decoration).
Steps for the Danda: 1. Find a Stick. 2. Smoothen the Surface. 3. Decorate (Optional).
Steps for the Gilli: 1. Find a Small Stick (about 3–4 inches long). 2. Shape the Ends (sharpen both ends). 3. Smoothen the Surface. 4. Decorate (Optional).
Final Touch: Test the gilli and danda to ensure they are easy to handle and sturdy.

B. Spellathon: How many words of three or more letters can you make from the letters shown in the following wheel? Rule: One letter can be used as many times as it appears in the wheel. Letters: A, C, E, T, H, E, R. For example: Tea, Reach. Can you make a seven-letter word using all the letters in the wheel? Now, create a letter wheel similar to the above.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Letter wheel with A, C, E, T, H, E, R for word-making game

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Just for Fun — Riddle: It belongs to you. But, your friends use it more than you. What is it?
